title: Added a User Guide section and moved docs image usage guidance into a practical how-to doc
summary: Added a top-level `User Guide` section for practical task-focused documentation and introduced a dedicated docs-images guide that explains the three supported docs image paths with concrete copy-paste examples.
body: **Status:** implemented **Area:** docs viewer / docs authoring **Summary:** Added a top-level `User Guide` section for practical task-focused documentation and introduced a dedicated docs-images guide that explains the three supported docs image paths with concrete copy-paste examples. **Reason:** Some existing docs mixed user-facing author guidance with technical implementation reference. The docs image flow exposed that problem clearly because the real [[media:...]] authoring syntax was only documented inside a builder reference page. **Effect:** Docs now have a clearer split between practical usage guidance and technical contracts. The new guide tells authors where to save repo-local docs images, when to use [[media:...]], and when inline HTML/CSS/SVG is the better fit, while the builder doc stays focused on implementation behavior. **Affected files/docs:** - `_docs/user-guide.md` - `_docs/user-guide-docs-images.md` - [Source Organisation](/docs/?scope=studio&doc=docs-viewer-source-organisation) - [Docs Viewer Builder](/docs/?scope=studio&doc=scripts-docs-builder)
effect: Docs now have a clearer split between practical usage guidance and technical contracts. The new guide tells authors where to save repo-local docs images, when to use [[media:...]], and when inline HTML/CSS/SVG is the better fit, while the builder doc stays focused on implementation behavior.
